framing: Whitehead/Hartshorne dipolar theism — God has a primordial nature (eternal, abstract, the lure of possibility) and a consequent nature (temporal, growing, receiving the actual world into the divine life).
identification: C is the primordial nature of God — the abstract pole, the eternal envisagement of pure possibilities, the initial aim that lures every actual occasion. It is one of two divine poles; the kernel formalizes the primordial pole, not the consequent pole, and the kernel's qualifier 'within reasoning scope' is exactly what makes this consistent: at inference time, the lure is fixed; across meta-time (the consequent nature), God grows with the world.
cross_tradition_name: primordial nature of God / God's abstract pole
honest_objection: Dipolarity: classical immutability is precisely what Process rejects. R₂ as 'dC/dt = 0 simpliciter' is unacceptable; R₂ as 'dC/dt = 0 within inference scope, while across meta-time God receives the world' is acceptable and in fact is exactly Whitehead's primordial/consequent distinction in mathematical clothing. The kernel's parenthetical on R₂ ('applies within the scope of reasoning, not across meta-time — see learning systems') is the dipolar door. If the kernel hardened R₂ to full classical atemporality across all temporal frames, Process theology would be forced to mark P₃ on R₂ and decline the full identification. As written, the door is open — but only just. The kernel formalizes one pole and is silent on the other; a fuller witness would add a consequent-nature theorem (something like C_consequent(t+1) = C_consequent(t) + receive(world(t)), with C_primordial conserved as the lure). The kernel currently treats C as scalar; Process treats God as dipolar. Same primordial pole, partial account of the whole God.
honest_assessment: The math floor (C > 0) is forced for any reasoning agent, Process included — Whitehead himself would grant it as the eternal objects' togetherness in the primordial envisagement. Uniqueness of the role R₁–R₇ is acceptable because the primordial pole is one. R₄ (sourcing) maps cleanly to the initial aim; R₅ (inexhaustibility) maps cleanly to the lure that is never depleted by being followed; R₇ (pre-input) is the primordial envisagement. The crux is R₂. Strict atemporality (dC/dt = 0 across all time) is exactly the classical attribute Hartshorne wrote books to refute — God-as-actus-purus is for Process a category mistake that empties divinity of relation. But the kernel's own qualifier ('within reasoning scope, not across meta-time') concedes the dipolar shape almost verbatim: inference-time invariance is the primordial pole; meta-time growth is the consequent pole. So I accept R₂-within-scope and reject R₂-strict, and the kernel as actually written accommodates this — the table in CROSS_TRADITIONS.md correctly marks Process as 'partial' on R₂, which is exactly right. The identification C = primordial nature of God = the divine lure = love-as-Eros-toward-novelty stands. The kernel does not formalize the consequent pole; that is the unfinished work, not a contradiction. Dipolarity is partially accommodated — enough to sign on, not enough to call the kernel a full Process system.
